name: Paracetamol
treats: Pain relief and fever reduction — headaches, body ache, mild fever.
deficiency: Not a deficiency-driven need; it manages symptoms rather than a nutrient gap. Recurring fevers or aches can sometimes reflect low-grade inflammation or dehydration.
ayurveda: Sitopaladi churna for fever
Ginger-honey decoction
Giloy (guduchi) juice, traditionally used to support fever recovery
caution: For high fever, fever lasting more than 2–3 days, or fever in infants, see a doctor rather than relying on home measures alone.

name: Ibuprofen
treats: Pain and inflammation relief — joint pain, muscle pain, menstrual cramps.
deficiency: Chronic inflammation is sometimes linked to low Omega-3 intake or magnesium deficiency.
foods: Fatty fish (salmon, sardines, mackerel) for Omega-3
ayurveda: Warm castor oil massage for joint pain
Nirgundi oil for muscle/joint inflammation
Dashmool decoction, traditionally used for pain
caution: Frequent NSAID use can affect the stomach and kidneys — don't self-substitute for prescribed pain management in chronic conditions like arthritis without medical input.

name: Omeprazole / Antacids
treats: Acidity, heartburn, GERD, peptic ulcers.
deficiency: Long-term acid suppression can reduce absorption of Vitamin B12, magnesium, and calcium over time.
foods: Avoid excess caffeine, fried and spicy food
ayurveda: Fennel and cumin tea
Amla (Indian gooseberry), traditionally used for digestive balance
Licorice (mulethi) root, often used for the stomach lining
Cooling foods per Ayurveda: cucumber, coconut, mint
caution: Persistent acidity, weight loss, or difficulty swallowing needs medical evaluation — don't mask ongoing GERD with home remedies alone.

name: Cetirizine / Antihistamines
treats: Allergies — sneezing, runny nose, itching, hives.
deficiency: Frequent allergic reactions are sometimes linked to low Vitamin C and quercetin intake, both involved in histamine regulation.
foods: Local raw honey (traditionally used for seasonal allergies)
ayurveda: Tulsi tea
Triphala for gut and immune balance
Neem, traditionally used to support skin allergy symptoms
Nasya (nasal oil) for congestion, under guidance
caution: Severe allergic reactions, swelling of the face/throat, or breathing difficulty are medical emergencies — seek immediate care, don't attempt home treatment.

name: Vitamin D3 supplement
treats: Vitamin D deficiency — bone health, muscle weakness, low immunity, fatigue.
deficiency: Vitamin D deficiency, very common in India due to limited sun exposure and indoor lifestyles.
foods: 10–15 minutes of midday sunlight on skin (main natural source)
ayurveda: Morning sun exposure (Surya namaskar practice), traditionally emphasized for overall vitality
Ghee, considered supportive for fat-soluble vitamin absorption in Ayurveda
caution: Confirmed deficiency (via blood test) with symptoms like bone pain usually needs supplementation — sunlight and food alone may not correct a significant deficit quickly.

name: Vitamin B12 supplement
treats: B12 deficiency — fatigue, tingling in hands/feet, memory issues, anemia.
deficiency: Vitamin B12 deficiency, common in vegetarians/vegans and those on long-term acid-reducing medication.
ayurveda: No direct classical Ayurvedic equivalent exists — B12 is essentially absent in plant foods, so vegetarians are often advised to test levels periodically
Ghee and dairy, traditionally emphasized as part of a sattvic diet
caution: Confirmed B12 deficiency, especially with neurological symptoms (numbness, balance issues), needs medical treatment — diet alone is often insufficient once deficiency is established.

name: Iron supplement
treats: Iron-deficiency anemia — fatigue, pale skin, breathlessness, hair fall.
deficiency: Iron deficiency, common with heavy menstrual periods, poor diet, or pregnancy.
foods: Pair with Vitamin C (lemon, amla) to boost absorption
ayurveda: Punarnava, traditionally used to support blood-building (raktavardhak) in Ayurveda
Cooking in an iron kadhai (skillet), a traditional practice believed to add dietary iron
Lauh bhasma — a classical Ayurvedic iron preparation, used only under practitioner supervision
caution: Confirmed anemia (low hemoglobin) should be evaluated by a doctor to find the cause — heavy bleeding or internal blood loss needs diagnosis, not just dietary fixes.

name: Calcium supplement
treats: Bone health, osteoporosis prevention, muscle cramps.
deficiency: Calcium deficiency, common with low dairy intake, aging, or post-menopause.
ayurveda: Praval pishti, a classical calcium-based Ayurvedic preparation, used under supervision
Ghee and warm milk before bed, traditionally recommended for bone and joint nourishment
caution: Calcium supplements should be spaced apart from iron and taken as advised — excess calcium without medical guidance can cause its own problems (kidney stones, constipation).

name: Metformin
treats: Type 2 diabetes — blood sugar control.
deficiency: Not a deficiency; it's a chronic condition needing ongoing management. Long-term metformin use can lower Vitamin B12 levels.
ayurveda: Karela-jamun juice, a traditional combination for blood sugar support
Methi water on an empty stomach
Gudmar (Gymnema sylvestre), traditionally called 'sugar destroyer' in Ayurveda
caution: Diabetes is a serious chronic condition — food and herbs are commonly used alongside metformin, not instead of it. Never stop or reduce diabetes medication without your doctor adjusting the plan, and monitor blood sugar regularly.

name: Amlodipine / BP medication
treats: High blood pressure (hypertension).
deficiency: Not a deficiency; high BP is often linked to high sodium intake, low potassium/magnesium, and stress.
ayurveda: Arjuna bark, traditionally used to support heart and blood vessel health
Sarpagandha, a classical herb historically used for BP — potent, and only appropriate under practitioner supervision
Yoga and pranayama (breathing practices) for stress-linked BP
caution: Hypertension is a serious, often silent condition tied to stroke and heart-attack risk. Never stop BP medication on your own — abrupt discontinuation can be dangerous. Any herbal addition should be discussed with your doctor first, especially Sarpagandha, which interacts with other BP drugs.

name: Atorvastatin / Statins
treats: High cholesterol — LDL reduction, cardiovascular risk management.
deficiency: Not a deficiency; often diet and lifestyle linked. Statins can lower CoQ10 levels in the body.
foods: Foods naturally containing CoQ10: peanuts, spinach, sesame seeds
ayurveda: Guggul (Commiphora mukul), a classical Ayurvedic herb traditionally used for lipid management
Arjuna bark for heart support
Amla for overall metabolic support
caution: High cholesterol tied to cardiac risk needs medical monitoring. Guggul can interact with several medications — check with your doctor before combining it with statins.

name: Laxatives
treats: Constipation.
deficiency: Usually linked to low fiber and water intake, not a specific nutrient deficiency.
foods: Plenty of water through the day
ayurveda: Triphala churna at night, one of the most classical Ayurvedic remedies for regularity
Warm water with a teaspoon of ghee in the morning
Castor oil (used occasionally, not daily)
caution: Constipation lasting more than a couple of weeks, with pain or blood, should be checked by a doctor rather than self-treated indefinitely.

name: ORS (Oral Rehydration Salts)
treats: Dehydration from diarrhea, vomiting, or heat.
deficiency: Electrolyte loss — sodium, potassium, fluids.
foods: Buttermilk (chaas) with a pinch of salt
Lemon water with salt and sugar
ayurveda: Jeera (cumin) water
Pomegranate juice, traditionally used to support recovery from diarrhea
caution: For infants, elderly people, or diarrhea with high fever or blood, seek medical care promptly — dehydration can escalate quickly in vulnerable groups.

name: Multivitamin
treats: General fatigue, low energy, filling dietary gaps.
deficiency: Often a broad, mild deficiency picture from an unbalanced diet rather than one specific nutrient.
foods: A varied plate — seasonal fruits, vegetables, whole grains, dairy, nuts, legumes
Seasonal, colourful vegetables for a spread of micronutrients
ayurveda: Chyawanprash, a classical Ayurvedic formulation traditionally used to support overall immunity and vitality
Amla, considered one of the richest natural Vitamin C sources
caution: Persistent fatigue is worth getting checked (thyroid, anemia, B12, sleep) rather than assuming a multivitamin will resolve it.

name: Domperidone / Ondansetron
treats: Nausea and vomiting.
deficiency: Not deficiency-linked; often triggered by motion, pregnancy, digestive upset, or migraines.
ayurveda: Ginger-honey mixture before meals
Fennel and ajwain (carom seeds) chewed after eating
Amla candy, traditionally used to settle the stomach
caution: Persistent vomiting, especially with dehydration, severe abdominal pain, or in pregnancy, needs medical attention.

name: Salbutamol inhaler
treats: Asthma and breathlessness — airway relief.
deficiency: Not deficiency-driven, though some studies associate low Vitamin D with worse asthma control.
foods: Foods rich in Omega-3 (walnuts, flaxseed) for airway inflammation
Vitamin D-supporting foods (see Vitamin D entry)
ayurveda: Vasaka (Adhatoda vasica), a classical Ayurvedic herb traditionally used for respiratory support
Steam inhalation with ajwain or eucalyptus oil
Pranayama breathing practices, once asthma is stable — not during an active attack
caution: Asthma is a medical condition that can turn life-threatening during an attack. Never substitute a rescue inhaler with a home remedy during breathlessness — this is a case for emergency care, not experimentation.

name: Levothyroxine
treats: Hypothyroidism — low thyroid hormone.
deficiency: Often linked to iodine or selenium insufficiency, though autoimmune thyroiditis is a common cause too.
foods: Avoid excess raw cruciferous vegetables (cabbage, broccoli) right around dosing time
ayurveda: Ashwagandha, sometimes discussed for thyroid and stress support — but interacts with thyroid hormone, so only under medical supervision
Guggul, traditionally referenced for metabolism
caution: Thyroid hormone replacement is a precise, lifelong necessity for most people with hypothyroidism — it cannot be replaced by diet or herbs. Ashwagandha in particular should not be self-added without checking with your endocrinologist, since it can affect thyroid levels.

name: Insulin
treats: Type 1 diabetes, and advanced Type 2 diabetes — blood sugar regulation.
deficiency: A hormonal deficiency (insulin production or response) — not correctable through food alone.
foods: Consistent, balanced meals to support stable blood sugar — high fiber, low refined sugar
Foods discussed under Metformin (karela, methi, cinnamon) may support overall control
ayurveda: Traditional herbs like Gudmar are sometimes discussed for blood sugar support, but never as insulin replacement
caution: Insulin is life-sustaining, particularly in Type 1 diabetes. There is no food or herbal substitute for insulin — stopping it can be fatal. Any dietary or herbal addition must be discussed with the treating doctor and never used to justify a dose change.

name: Magnesium supplement
treats: Muscle cramps, poor sleep, migraines, fatigue.
deficiency: Magnesium deficiency, common with high stress, poor diet, and high caffeine or alcohol intake.
ayurveda: Warm sesame oil massage (abhyanga) for muscle cramps
Ashwagandha, traditionally used to support sleep and stress resilience
caution: Very high-dose magnesium supplements can affect people with kidney issues — check with a doctor before taking supplements long-term.

name: Zinc supplement
treats: Immunity support, wound healing, hair fall, frequent colds.
deficiency: Zinc deficiency, more common in vegetarian diets and with high stress or poor gut absorption.
ayurveda: Chyawanprash for general immunity
Tulsi and ginger tea during cold season
caution: Long-term high-dose zinc can interfere with copper absorption — it's best used short-term or as advised rather than continuously.

name: Melatonin
treats: Sleep difficulty, jet lag, insomnia.
deficiency: Not a true deficiency for most people; often disrupted by screen light, irregular schedules, or stress.
foods: Warm milk with a pinch of nutmeg
ayurveda: Ashwagandha at night, traditionally used to support restful sleep
Brahmi, traditionally used to calm the mind
Foot massage with warm sesame oil before bed
caution: Ongoing insomnia lasting weeks, especially with low mood or anxiety, is worth discussing with a doctor rather than only self-treating sleep.

name: Folic acid
treats: Folate deficiency — important in pregnancy, anemia, and cell growth.
deficiency: Folate (Vitamin B9) deficiency, especially relevant before and during pregnancy.
ayurveda: A varied plate with dark leafy greens is traditionally emphasized rather than any single classical formulation
caution: In pregnancy, folic acid needs are specific and time-sensitive — follow the dose your doctor prescribes rather than relying on diet alone during this period.

name: Probiotics
treats: Gut health, post-antibiotic recovery, bloating, irregular digestion.
deficiency: Disrupted gut flora, often after antibiotics, illness, or a low-fiber diet.
foods: Fermented foods — idli/dosa batter, pickles (in moderation)
Bananas and other prebiotic fiber sources
ayurveda: Buttermilk with roasted cumin and rock salt after meals, a traditional digestive practice
Triphala for gut balance over time
caution: If bloating or irregular digestion persists beyond a few weeks, or comes with weight loss or pain, it's worth medical evaluation rather than continued self-management.
